methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $510K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
GenFab Co is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $510K to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of nine private robotics peers and one public comparable, though narrow peer matching at the seed stage constrains available methodologies.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, using total capital raised as a proxy for seed-stage value. Applying a typical 2.5x multiplier from seed to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $1.275M. This method grounds the analysis in observable company metrics and standard step-up conventions for early-stage robotics ventures. Peer-median triangulation could not be applied; while 11 total robotics and manufacturing peers exist in the broader comparison set, the seed stage filters identified zero direct same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum statistical thresholds (3+ datapoints), making a median approach unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.08M to $1.47M, with a median point estimate of $1.28M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression method. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer comparables at the seed stage, limiting external validation; the early maturity of the company and absence of operational milestones in the provided data; and broader robotics sector valuation volatility. Refinement would benefit from additional seed-stage robotics datapoints or disclosed metrics on user traction, revenue, or IP differentiation.
